text: We're going to walk through how to give critical feedback to someone on your team without crushing their spirit or making things awkward. Let's start with a simple example. Imagine you're pulling someone aside and saying, Hey, I just wanted to have a quick chat. You've been doing some fantastic work lately. You're a valuable part of the team and I really appreciate what you bring.

I know that's not your usual self and I know you're capable of more, so I'd Love to work with you on this. Can we come up with a few ideas to help improve that? Here's why it works. First, you lead with something positive, something real that's not just fluff. It helps the person Feel seen and valued. Then You deliver the part that needs attention, the critique, not criticism, not a personal attack,

You finish with an offer to work together on a solution. You're not just pointing out a fault. You're saying, let's fix this together. This structure is sometimes called the feedback sandwich.
